subject: deselected frames still contributing to downstream processing
body:
Hi Everyone,

Before I run tilt-series alignment, I always manually examine the tilt 
stack for bad tilts (i.e. ice coming into view, separate from Warp's 
metric-based selection. I list the frame series from which the bad tilts 
originate in a text file and ensure that the text file will point to the 
directories where the frameseries .xml files are listed. In deselected 
frameseries xml files, I can confirm that manual deselction has occurred. 

In this example frameseries xml file 
(TS_001_043_-50.00_20250329_090548_fractions.xml):
?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Movie DataDirectory="20250329_frames" UnselectFilter="False" UnselectManual
="True" CTFResolutionEstimate="11.4" MeanFrameMovement="0.0002054361" 
MaskPercentage="-1" Bfactor="0" Weight="1" MagnificationCorrection="1, 0, 
0, 1">

Despite this deselection, I can see that the tiltseries xml file still 
selects that specific frameseries to contribute to the tiltseries.

This is a common issue across all my deselected frameseries. The issue 
isn't fixed even when I nuke the tiltseries directory and tomostar 
directories to rerun subsequent steps. I'm ok with manually editing the xml 
file to confirm the deselection for now, but has anyone also come across 
and fixed this issue?

Best,
Daniel
